reason: The single residual finding is low impact. The omitted table names (SYSTEM_ACCOUNT, SYSTEM_ACCOUNT_AUTHORITY, UGROUP_SYSTEM_ACCOUNT, USERS) are specific to the sample implementation (ユーザ情報削除バッチ), not to the DB-input batch pattern being taught. The knowledge file's purpose is to illustrate the BatchAction pattern (initialize/createReader/handle), which is fully covered. An AI helping a developer implement a DB-input batch has no need for the sample's specific table names. The processing flow in s3 already states "各テーブルからユーザ情報を削除" at the appropriate level of abstraction.

findings_assessment.location: s3 (１件ごとの処理) — handle method Javadoc
findings_assessment.justification: The four table names are sample-specific implementation details of the example ユーザ情報削除バッチ, not part of the DB-input batch framework pattern. The knowledge file teaches the pattern (BatchAction lifecycle, exclusive control, TransactionAbnormalEnd usage), not the sample's schema. A developer asking how to build a DB-input batch gains nothing from knowing which tables the example deletes. Phase E did not fix this because the agent correctly judged the Javadoc table list as example-specific noise rather than pattern knowledge — the s3 section content is otherwise unchanged between Phase D and Phase E outputs.
